weeks.title: Authentication, Security, Deployment & Production Engineering

topics.title: Authentication in Next.js (Production Patterns)
points: NextAuth.js / Auth.js v5: adapter pattern, OAuth providers, credentials, JWT vs database sessions
Clerk: managed auth — faster to ship, less control. Know when to use managed vs custom.
Session management: JWT stored in httpOnly cookie (XSS safe). Server components read session without API call.
Route protection: middleware.ts — check session cookie, redirect to /login if missing. Don't rely on client-side auth guards alone.
Role-based access control (RBAC): encode roles in JWT, check in middleware AND server components AND UI
Refresh token rotation: short-lived access token (15min), long-lived refresh token (7 days), automatic rotation
OAuth flow: redirect to provider → callback URL → exchange code for tokens → create session
PKCE (Proof Key for Code Exchange): prevents auth code interception attacks in OAuth

topics.title: Frontend Security
points: XSS (Cross-Site Scripting): injecting malicious scripts. React's JSX escapes by default — dangerouslySetInnerHTML is the escape hatch, sanitize all inputs with DOMPurify.
CSRF (Cross-Site Request Forgery): forged requests from another origin. Next.js Server Actions have CSRF protection built in. For REST APIs: use SameSite=Strict cookies or CSRF tokens.
CORS: server controls which origins can make requests. Not a client-side security measure — it's a browser enforcement of server policy.
Content Security Policy (CSP): HTTP header that restricts which resources can load. Prevents XSS damage even if attack gets through.
Sensitive data in client: NEVER put secrets, API keys, or sensitive data in client-side code or localStorage.
Dependency security: npm audit regularly, Dependabot alerts, lock file for reproducible builds.

topics.title: Next.js Deployment & Production
points: Vercel: zero-config deployment for Next.js. Automatic CDN, edge functions, preview deployments.
Self-hosted: next start (Node.js server), docker container, or standalone output mode
Environment variables: server-only (.env), client-exposed (NEXT_PUBLIC_ prefix) — never expose secrets with NEXT_PUBLIC_
CI/CD: GitHub Actions → lint → test → build → deploy. Must fail on TypeScript errors and failing tests.
Error monitoring: Sentry — capture and track production errors with stack traces, user context, release tracking.
Analytics: Vercel Analytics or PostHog — track Core Web Vitals from real users (RUM)
Feature flags: LaunchDarkly or simple env var strategy — release features without redeploys

knowledge_check: What is the difference between JWT and database sessions in NextAuth? When would you choose each?
How do you implement route protection in Next.js? Why is middleware better than client-side auth guards?
What is CSRF? Does React protect against it automatically? What does Next.js Server Actions do to prevent CSRF?
What is dangerouslySetInnerHTML? When might you need it and how do you use it safely?
What environment variables are exposed to the client in Next.js? How do you prevent accidental secret exposure?
Implement RBAC in Next.js: admin can access /admin, users cannot. Cover middleware AND server component level.
What is refresh token rotation? Why is it more secure than long-lived access tokens?
Set up a CI/CD pipeline with GitHub Actions for a Next.js app. What jobs do you run and in what order?
How do you add Sentry to a Next.js App Router project? What configuration do you need for both server and client errors?
What is Content Security Policy? Write a basic CSP header for a Next.js app that uses external fonts and Stripe.

weeks.title: Interview Preparation, Portfolio Polish, Mock Interviews
weeks.overview: You've done the work. Now you need to be able to PERFORM under interview pressure. Week 8 is about converting your knowledge into offers.

days.title: Interview Strategy + Resume Rewrite

topics.title: Resume Upgrade for Senior Frontend Roles
points: Current gap: your resume reads like a mid-level backend developer who sometimes touches React. Rewrite it as a frontend specialist.
Headline: "Frontend Engineer | React · Next.js · TypeScript · Real-Time UI" — right at the top, not buried in skills
Skills reorder: Frontend (React, Next.js, TypeScript, TanStack Query, Zustand, RHF, WebSocket) FIRST. Then backend. Then tools.
Bullet formula: Verb + What + How + Result. "Architected" is better than "Built". Quantify everything.
PharmaSync: rewrite to highlight React architecture, TypeScript, performance patterns — not just features
New entries: add your 60-day portfolio projects with GitHub links and live URLs
Remove or downplay: n8n automation (not frontend), Python (not frontend), generic "Built features" bullets

topics.title: Technical Interview Patterns
points: React internals questions: Fiber, reconciliation, hooks linked list — you know these cold now
Performance questions: always start with "measure first" then walk through systematic optimization
TypeScript questions: generics, discriminated unions, conditional types — practice on TypeHero
System design: use the framework — requirements, component tree, state architecture, data flow, performance, real-time
Take-home projects: treat them as production code — TypeScript, tests, README, performance, a11y
Code review rounds: show you understand the existing code, suggest improvements with reasoning
Behavioral: STAR format. For you: the startup shutdown story must become "I used this opportunity to level up" narrative — not a sad story, a transformation story.

topics.title: Interview Questions You Will Be Asked
points: "Explain how React renders" → Fiber, work loop, render phase, commit phase — 5-minute answer
"Why does React have hooks instead of classes?" → Function components + hooks are simpler, composable, no this binding issues
"How do you optimize a slow React app?" → Profile first, identify cause, systematic fixes
"What is the difference between SSR and SSG and ISR in Next.js?" → nail all 3 with use cases
"How does Next.js App Router differ from Pages Router?" → RSC, server actions, layout system
"Design a real-time chat system" → your Week 6 knowledge
"Explain your biggest technical challenge and how you solved it" → ready 3 stories

end_goals.closing_message: Raghav, you have one job. Execute. Do not overthink. Do not switch plans. Do not skip days. Every day: 8 hours. Every knowledge check: answer every question. Every project: ship it live with a link. The startup closing down is not your story. It's just context. Your story is what you do starting today. You have the foundation. You have the discipline (9.0 CGPA proves it). You have the real experience. Now go build the version of yourself that companies fight to hire.
